status: P1 EVIDENCE CLOSURE WAVE 5 / IDENTITY OR PRODUCT-EQUIVALENCE BLOCK ACTIVE / NOT PLATINUM CERTIFIED
classification: Chemically unresolved glucagon-like peptide-1 supplier product family
mechanism_summary: No product-specific mechanism is assigned. Canonical active human forms include GLP-1(7-36) amide and GLP-1(7-37), but the catalogue does not establish which molecular form, sequence, terminus, salt or analogue is supplied.
evidence_maturity: Human physiology and infusion evidence exist for exact native GLP-1 molecular forms. Those data cannot be attributed to the supplier vial before chemical identity closure.
regulatory_headline: CRITICAL MOLECULAR-IDENTITY GAP / NO NATIVE-FORM, ANALOGUE, APPROVAL OR THERAPEUTIC-EQUIVALENCE CLAIM
certification_blockers: Exact peptide sequence and residue range
Product-specific human evidence and regulatory status
closure_class: CRITICAL NATIVE GLP-1 MOLECULAR FORM AND PRODUCT IDENTITY
closure_state: BLOCKED — RESIDUE RANGE, C-TERMINAL FORM, ANALOGUE STATUS, SALT, POTENCY, ROUTE, STERILITY AND PRODUCT-SPECIFIC HUMAN EVIDENCE UNVERIFIED
authoritative_product_boundary: Human studies distinguish GLP-1(7-36) amide and GLP-1(7-37), both of which have very short plasma exposure and defined route-specific physiology. The GP listing does not identify either form or exclude a precursor, metabolite or modified analogue. FDA's 2026 enforcement position also separates non-approved GLP-1 products from exact approved active ingredients and products.
critical_closure_controls: Do not assign GP to GLP-1(7-36) amide
Do not assign GP to GLP-1(7-37)
No semaglutide, liraglutide or other analogue evidence transfer
Native GLP-1 human physiology is not supplier-product validation
No glucose-control, weight-loss, dosing, injection or reconstitution protocol
